signals.raw:
TRUMP ANNOUNCES TWO-WEEK CEASEFIRE WITH IRAN AHEAD OF 8 P.M. DEADLINE | YES | HIGH confidence**

President Trump announced a **two-week ceasefire agreement with Iran** on Tuesday evening, just before a critical deadline.[1][2] This followed an extraordinary escalation in rhetoric earlier that day, where Trump threatened Iran with "annihilation" and warned that "a whole civilization will die tonigh

signals.headline: South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026, up from 2.0% in prior months but below 2.4% forecasts | NO | HIGH confidence**[1][3]

Bank of Korea confirmed 7th consecutive interest rate freeze at 2.5% on April 10, 2026, citing March's 2.2% inflation and won volatility amid Middle East tensions, ruling out cuts despite contained pressures[1][3]. This aligns with prior moderation trends

signals.headline: **Strait of Hormuz remains closed:** Despite the ceasefire, Iran has not agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which ha
signals.raw: **Strait of Hormuz remains closed:** Despite the ceasefire, Iran has not agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which handles roughly 20 percent of the world's oil supply and has driven fuel prices higher across Europe and the U.S.[2]

signals.raw: **Threat of escalation:** Trump has threatened to strike Iranian civilian infrastructure—including bridges, power plants, and desalination plants—if the strait remains closed, raising concerns about potential violations of international law.[2]

signals.headline: **Nuclear concerns cited:** Trump framed the conflict in existential terms, stating Iran "is going to be a nuclear natio
signals.raw:
**Nuclear concerns cited:** Trump framed the conflict in existential terms, stating Iran "is going to be a nuclear nation so they can exterminate the world" and asserting "not going to happen."[4]

The two-week ceasefire represents a temporary de-escalation, but the underlying disputes over the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's nuclear program remain unresolved.
